channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Waste Bags Hazardous, 22nd May
item.description:
Waste bags left near the Admaston Road entrance to Shrewsbury Park

item.title: J/W 19 Morning lane, 22nd May
item.description:
Reported litter and dead rat j/w 19 Morning Lane, The Gabba building. dead mice near zebra crossing
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chatham Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegal parking, 22nd May
item.description:
This car is regularly parking in this bay and for the entire day. The person is using a disabled badge to avoid paying. This person appears to be leaving the car to go and work in the area. Please can this be investigated?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland High Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown privet bush, 22nd May
item.description:
Overgrown privet bush overhanging the pavement, such that people have to dodge the bush. Especially troublesome/dangerous for blind people and those with push chairs.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Talbot Road, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 22nd May
item.description:
There is a dead fox that needs collecting and disposing. It will soon start to smell in this heat
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Manor Place, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Heavily polluted street by large items/ litter, 22nd May
item.description:
Street again HEAVILY polluted by household rubbish ( large items ) all over caused by SOCIAL HOUSING!!!!! Please get this cleaned before Bank Holiday weekend as we have to live in this GHETTO thanks to SOCIAL HOUSING!!! I am reporting this ongoing issue on to local Councillors. Thank tou
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maiden Lane, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Litter bin overflowing

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woodhill, Woolwich

item.title: By victoria mews bins, 22nd May
item.description:
A crate of strawberries has been dumped by victoria mews bins
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Mews, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Urgent Safety Concern Near Schools: Used Razor Blades and Commercial Waste Scattered on Pavement in East Dulwich, 22nd May
item.description:
I want to raise an ongoing public safety concern on the road near East Dulwich station, close to Goose Green Primary School and the nearby secondary school/academy. Outside a salon believed to be Tristan’s Beauty Salon, rubbish bags are regularly being left on the pavement or in an unsecured bin opposite the salon. The bags appear to contain mixed waste including food waste, hair clippings, and used razor blades/shaving blades. At night, foxes are ripping the bags open and spreading the contents across the pavement and street. This has happened repeatedly over a number of weeks, not just once. I have personally seen large numbers of used Wilkinson-style razor blades scattered across the pavement mixed with hair and rubbish. This is extremely concerning given the number of children and families walking through the area every day to and from Goose Green Primary School and the nearby secondary school. A child could easily pick up or step on one of these blades. Please can this be investigated urgently and the business spoken to regarding proper commercial waste disposal procedures. Sharp items such as used razor blades should be placed in a sealed, secure sharps container and kept separate from food waste and general rubbish. Waste bags should also be secured properly so they cannot be torn open by animals. This is a recurring health and safety issue and needs immediate attention before someone is injured.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent Safety Concern Near Schools: Used Razor Blades and Commercial Waste Scattered on Pavement in East Dulwich, 22nd May
item.description:
I want to raise an ongoing public safety concern on the road near East Dulwich station, close to Goose Green Primary School and the nearby secondary school/academy. Outside a salon believed to be Tristan’s Beauty Salon, rubbish bags are regularly being left on the pavement or in an unsecured bin opposite the salon. The bags appear to contain mixed waste including food waste, hair clippings, and used razor blades/shaving blades. At night, foxes are ripping the bags open and spreading the contents across the pavement and street. This has happened repeatedly over a number of weeks, not just once. I have personally seen large numbers of used Wilkinson-style razor blades scattered across the pavement mixed with hair and rubbish. This is extremely concerning given the number of children and families walking through the area every day to and from Goose Green Primary School and the nearby secondary school. A child could easily pick up or step on one of these blades. Please can this be investigated urgently and the business spoken to regarding proper commercial waste disposal procedures. Sharp items such as used razor blades should be placed in a sealed, secure sharps container and kept separate from food waste and general rubbish. Waste bags should also be secured properly so they cannot be torn open by animals. This is a recurring health and safety issue and needs immediate attention before someone is injured.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
